mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean performance during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with declining real savings flows indicate tightening financial conditions that suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS declines and FEDFUNDS rises, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following industrial production drops, or the predictive power vanishes during periods of stable manufacturing output.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust economic growth that supports corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day equity returns, or the signal reverses direction during periods of wage inflation without productivity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 2 million units signals a collapse in residential investment demand that precedes broader economic contraction, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for 60 days following housing starts below 2000k, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for mortgage rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation alongside decelerating real GDP growth creates a stagflationary environment that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPI and falling GDP growth yields positive equity returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in real household savings rates during periods of economic uncertainty indicate precautionary hoarding that reduces immediate consumption, causing short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn positive when savings rates spike, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of stable consumer confidence.
